# Part 5 — The Rules of Electricity {.sdaia-dark background-gradient="linear-gradient(135deg, #1C355E, #00C9A7)"}

## The Two Safety Rules {background-color="#f7f9fc"}

Before we touch anything, two rules that will save your robot every time:

::: {.incremental}

1. 🛡️ **Always use a resistor with an LED** — without one, too much current flows and the LED burns out
2. ⚡ **Never connect 5V to GND with a bare wire** — it's called a short circuit and it can fry your Arduino

:::

::: {.fragment}

::: {.callout-warning}

## The Finger Test
If a component feels **hot to the touch** — unplug immediately. Too much current is flowing somewhere.

:::

:::

## The Narrow Pipe Rule {background-color="#f7f9fc"}

::::: {.columns}

:::: {.column width="50%"}

::: {.incremental}

- A narrow pipe **slows down** water flow
- A **resistor** does the same thing for electricity
- The narrower (higher resistance) → the less current flows
- This *protects* LEDs from getting too much

:::

::::

:::: {.column width="50%"}

::: {.callout-tip}

## In the Kit
Your Keyestudio kit comes with **pre-selected resistors** for each LED. You don't need to calculate — just use the right colour band!

For the traffic light: **330 Ω** (orange-orange-brown) ✓

:::

::::

:::::

## ✋ Check-In {background-color="#00C9A7" .sdaia-dark}

::: {.r-fit-text}

How are you feeling?

👍 Got it! &nbsp;&nbsp; 🤔 A bit confused &nbsp;&nbsp; 🤯 Very lost

:::

*Tell the instructor — we'll move on when everyone's good.*

---

## 🔥 Bonus — Ohm's Law: V = I × R {background-color="#1C355E" .sdaia-dark}

> This section is for **students who want to go deeper**. Skip it if you're good with the rules above.

::: {.r-fit-text}

**V = I × R**

:::

## 🔥 Bonus — Three Forms of the Same Truth

You only need one formula — rearrange for what you don't know:

| Find | Formula | Units |
|------|---------|-------|
| **Voltage** | V = I × R | Volts (V) |
| **Current** | I = V / R | Amperes (A) |
| **Resistance** | R = V / I | Ohms (Ω) |

> If pressure goes up and resistance stays the same, flow increases.
> Same physics. Same formula.

## 🔥 Bonus — Ohm's Law Sandbox {background-color="#f7f9fc"}

<iframe data-src="../simulations/water_circuit/index.html"
        width="100%" height="520px"
        style="border:none; border-radius:12px;"></iframe>

*Calculate a target Current first. Then adjust the Voltage and Resistance sliders — does the gauge match your math?*

## 🔥 Bonus — Exercise: Calculate Current {background-color="#00C9A7" .sdaia-dark}

*(recall)* A battery provides **5 V**. A resistor of **100 Ω** is connected. How much current flows?

Show your calculation.

::: {.fragment}
**Answer:** I = V / R = 5 / 100 = **0.05 A (50 mA)**
:::

## 🔥 Bonus — Exercise: Find the Resistor {background-color="#00C9A7" .sdaia-dark}

*(apply)* An LED needs **2 V** and **20 mA**. Your Arduino gives **5 V**. What resistor do you need?

::: {.fragment}
**Answer:** V_resistor = 5 − 2 = **3 V** · R = 3 / 0.02 = **150 Ω** → pick **150 Ω or 220 Ω** ✓
:::
